basic_details.job_title: KEA AO AAO Recruitment 2026 Notification Out - Apply Online for 890 Agriculture Officer & Assistant Agriculture Officer Posts
basic_details.short_information: The Karnataka Examinations Authority (KEA) has officially announced the Agriculture Officer (AO) & Assistant Agriculture Officer (AAO) Recruitment 2026 with 872 + 18 (HK) vacancies. The detailed notification was released under reference numbers EDI/KEA/08/RPC/2026 & EDI/KEA/09/KK/2026 on the official website cetonline.karnataka.gov.in/kea/. Candidates who meet the age criteria of 40 to 45 years (category-wise relaxation applicable) and possess qualifications such as B.Sc (Agriculture) / B.Sc (Hons) Agriculture / relevant B.Tech or B.Sc degrees can submit their online applications from 19 April

age_limit.details: The age limit to apply for KEA Agriculture Officer & Assistant Agriculture Officer Recruitment 2026 is 42 years. Age relaxation will be provided to reserved category candidates as per government rules.

qualification.education_qualification: B.Sc (Agri/Hons) or specified B.Tech/B.Sc degrees (see Eligibility below)
qualification.details: Candidates must fulfil the essential educational qualification as on the last date of application. | Agriculture Officer & Assistant Agriculture Officer (85% posts) | Essential Educational Qualification: B.Sc (Agriculture) or B.Sc (Hons) Agriculture from ICAR/AICTE recognised university. | Agriculture Officer & Assistant Agriculture Officer (15% posts) | Essential Educational Qualification: B.Tech (Food Science & Technology) / B.Tech (Food Technology) / B.Sc (Sericulture & Cooperation) / B.Sc (Agri Marketing & Cooperation) / B.Sc (Agri Business Management) / B.Sc (Agri Biotechnology) / B.Tech (Biotechnology) / B.Sc (Agricultural Engineering) / B.Tech (Agricultural Engineering). | For All Posts | Other Conditions: Must be registered on OTR portal. Kannada language proficiency required (exemption for certain candidates). Only Indian citizens / eligible categories.

salary_details.details: Plus DA, HRA and other allowances as per rules. 2-year probation period. NPS applicable.

selection_process: Kannada Language Test (qualifying) + Written Exam (Offline OMR Objective Type - 2 papers
300 marks each). Negative marking: 0.75 marks per wrong answer. Minimum qualifying marks: 35% in written exam. Final selection on merit in written exam (after Kannada test clearance).
how_to_apply: Visit the official KEA website: https://cetonline.karnataka.gov.in/kea/
Click on the relevant recruitment notification link under Direct Recruitment section
Complete the online registration using valid mobile number and email ID
Fill the online application form with accurate personal, educational, and category details
Upload scanned photograph (50–200 KB) and signature (50–70 KB) in JPEG format
Upload required certificates such as education, caste, income, and other applicable documents
Pay the prescribed application fee through online mode (Debit Card/Credit Card/Net Banking/UPI)
Verify all entered details carefully before final submission of the application
Submit the application form online within the specified last date
Download and print the submitted application form and fee receipt for future reference
important_instructions: Only online applications will be accepted. No offline applications will be entertained. Candidates must register on OTR portal before filling application. Application fee once paid will not be refunded. Canvassing in any form will lead to disqualification. KEA reserves the right to cancel/modify the recruitment process at any stage.

faq.question: Q1. How many vacancies are there in KEA AO & AAO Recruitment 2026 and how are they split between RPC and Kalyana Karnataka?
faq.answer: KEA has released notifications EDI/KEA/08/RPC/2026 and EDI/KEA/09/KK/2026 for a total of 872 + 18 HK posts, with 630 + 02 (HK) posts under Residual Parent Cadre (RPC) and 242 + 16 (HK) posts under Kalyana Karnataka region for Agriculture Officer and Assistant Agriculture Officer in the Karnataka Agriculture Department.

faq.question: Q2. What are the online application dates and exam dates for KEA AO & AAO 2026?
faq.answer: The online application starts on 19 April 2026 and the last date to apply is 10 May 2026, while the last date to pay the application fee is 12 May 2026; the Kannada Language Test is scheduled on 06 June 2026, followed by the RPC written exam on 07 June 2026 and the KK written exam on 14 June 2026 as notified by KEA.

faq.question: Q3. How are the AO and AAO vacancies distributed between the 85% and 15% educational streams?
faq.answer: Out of the total posts, 85% are reserved for candidates with B.Sc (Agriculture) / B.Sc (Hons) Agriculture, and the remaining 15% are for candidates with specified B.Tech/B.Sc degrees such as Food Technology, Sericulture, Agri Marketing & Cooperation, Agri Business Management, Agri Biotechnology, Biotechnology, Agricultural Engineering, etc., with separate vacancy numbers for AO and AAO under RPC and KK as given in the notification tables.

faq.question: Q4. What is the educational qualification required for KEA Agriculture Officer & Assistant Agriculture Officer posts?
faq.answer: For 85% seats, candidates must have B.Sc (Agriculture) or B.Sc (Hons) Agriculture from an ICAR/AICTE recognised University. For 15% seats, acceptable degrees include B.Tech (Food Science & Technology/Food Technology/Biotechnology/Agricultural Engineering), B.Sc (Sericulture & Cooperation, Agri Marketing & Cooperation, Agri Business Management, Agri Biotechnology) or equivalent as specified in the advertisements.

faq.question: Q5. What is the age limit and relaxation for KEA AO & AAO Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The general upper age limit is 40 years for General Merit candidates, 43 years for Category 2A/2B/3A/3B, and 45 years for SC/ST/Category‑1 candidates, with the minimum age being 18 years as per Karnataka government norms on the cut‑off date.

faq.question: Q6. What is the application fee for KEA AO & AAO 2026?
faq.answer: The total fee is ₹750 for General, Cat‑2A, 2B, 3A, 3B candidates (₹500 application fee + ₹250 processing), ₹500 for SC/ST/Category‑1/Ex‑Servicemen/Transgender candidates (₹250 application fee + ₹250 processing), and ₹250 for PwD candidates (processing fee only), payable online via KEA portal.

faq.question: Q7. What is the selection process and exam pattern for KEA Agriculture Officer & Assistant Agriculture Officer?
faq.answer: The selection process comprises a Kannada Language Test (qualifying) followed by a competitive written exam in Offline OMR mode with two papers of 300 marks each, carrying objective‑type questions with 0.75 negative marking for each wrong answer, and candidates must secure at least 35% marks in the written exam to be considered for the merit list.

faq.question: Q8. Is Kannada language test compulsory for all candidates in KEA AO & AAO recruitment?
faq.answer: Yes, Kannada Language Test is compulsory for candidates who have not studied Kannada as a medium/subject in prescribed qualifying examinations, and it is qualifying in nature; exemptions are provided to those who meet the language criteria as detailed in the notification.

faq.question: Q9. What are the pay scales for KEA Agriculture Officer and Assistant Agriculture Officer posts?
faq.answer: As per the revised 2024 pay structure, Agriculture Officer posts carry a pay scale of ₹69,250 – ₹1,34,200, while Assistant Agriculture Officer posts carry ₹65,950 – ₹1,24,900, plus DA, HRA and other allowances as per Karnataka Government rules, with a 2‑year probation period and NPS (National Pension System) applicable.

faq.question: Q10. How can I apply online for KEA AO & AAO Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Eligible candidates must visit the KEA official website (cetonline.karnataka.gov.in/kea), register through the OTR (One Time Registration) system, select the relevant AO/AAO notification for RPC or Kalyana Karnataka, fill the online application with personal, educational and reservation details, upload required documents and photo/signature, pay the applicable fee online, and finally submit and download the application form for future reference before 10 May 2026.

faq.answer: KEA has released notifications EDI/KEA/08/RPC/2026 and EDI/KEA/09/KK/2026 for a total of 872 + 18 HK posts, with 630 + 02 (HK) posts under Residual Parent Cadre (RPC) and 242 + 16 (HK) posts under Kalyana Karnataka region for Agriculture Officer and Assistant Agriculture Officer in the Karnataka Agriculture Department.
